items.hook: The United Red Army is founded in Japan.
items.body: The United Red Army (URA) was a far-left militant organization that operated in Japan between July 1971 and March 1972. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Vietnam War: The United States and South Vietnam begin Operation Hastings to push the North Vietnamese out of the Vietnamese Demilitarized Zone.
items.body: The Vietnam War was an armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia fought between North Vietnam and South Vietnam and their allies. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Reconstruction Era of the United States: Georgia becomes the last of the former Confederate states to be readmitted to the Union.
items.body: The Reconstruction era, often simply called Reconstruction, was a period in United States history that followed the American Civil War (1861–1865) and was dominated by the legal, social, and political challenges of the abolition of slavery and federal control over, and reintegration of, the former Confederate States into the United States. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: UK crew unemployment rose as volume of film and TV releases fell 13% between 2022-2025
items.body: The production workforce was estimated to be working at about 50-55% capacity in 2025.

items.hook: WGA adds its own suit to Paramount-Warner merger opposition
items.body: The guild says the merger would create incentive and ability to suppress writers’ wages.

items.hook: Swedish–Novgorodian Wars: A Novgorodian army led by Alexander Nevsky defeats the Swedes in the Battle of the Neva.
items.body: The Swedish–Novgorodian Wars were a series of armed conflicts in the 12th and 13th centuries between the Novgorod Republic and medieval Sweden over control of the Gulf of Finland. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Robert Barbour Johnson (1907–1987) was an American artist and writer of weird fiction, whose stories were allegedly admired by H.
items.body: Johnson wrote "Far Below" (1939), voted in 1953 by readers as the best story ever published in Weird Tales magazine. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Apple’s ‘Wild Things’, ‘The Alex’, and Netflix’s ’The Boys From Berlin’ secure nearly €50m for German shoots
items.body: The German Motion Picture Fund awarded nearly 90% of the total €70m to US streaming projects in first six months of 2026.

items.hook: Walk of the Spirits is a 2008 American young adult romance and thriller novel written by author Richie Tankersley Cusick.
items.body: It is followed by her novel, Shadow Mirror. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: A Most Wanted Man is a thriller/espionage novel by British writer John le Carré, published in September 2008 by Hodder & Stoughton in the United Kingdom and in October 2008 by Scribner in the United States.
items.body: Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: To Love is a 1997 Japanese drama film written and directed by Kei Kumai.
items.body: It is based on the novel Watashi ga Suteta Onna by Shūsaku Endō, making it Kumai's third and final adaptation of an Endō novel. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Giffoni unveils competition line-up of 104 films exploring the fears, hopes and identities of new generations - Giffoni 2026
items.body: The Italian children and youth-orientated gathering has announced a line-up spanning coming-of-age fiction, animation, social drama and documentaries

items.hook: Judith Colelll is filming the historical drama Maestras - Production / Funding - Spain/Belgium
items.body: Bruna Cusí, Daniela Brown, Nausicaa Bonnín, Greta Fernández and Natalia de Molina star in a film about the clandestine struggle of the Republican school system during the Franco regime in Spain

items.hook: The Holocaust: Nazi Germany begins the deportation of 100,000 Jews from the occupied Netherlands to extermination camps.
items.body: The Holocaust, known in Hebrew as the Shoah, was the genocide of European Jews during World War II. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Historical records indicate that in the past, the Kong-kài served other functions, including a men's gathering place or a public meeting place for discussions and decision-making.
items.body: A Kong-kài, literally "the Public Hall" in Taiwanese Hokkien, is a temple or shrine where indigenous peoples like the Siraya, Taivoan or Makatao hold rituals for their ancestral spirits. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: A Belgian Air Force C-130 Hercules carrying the Royal Netherlands Army marching band crashes on landing at Eindhoven Airport.
items.body: The Belgian Air Force is the air arm of the Belgian Armed Forces. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Sajan KC is a Nepalese entomologist.
items.body: His research focuses on the taxonomy of beetles and butterflies across South Asia, particularly the Himalayas, and the Americas. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Interview: Élie Wajeman • Director of Comète - “Paris is still vibrant, dangerous, moving, and violent” - /France
items.body: The French filmmaker recounts the journey of an ensemble film that began as an alternative experiment in filmmaking and genre blending

items.hook: Hidden Creatures by Dino Martins review – the revolting world of parasites
items.body:
From maggots to viruses, this gross-out compendium also manages to celebrate the awe and inventiveness of nature
